violations.text: Observed two outside dumpsters missing covers. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered/closed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

The presence of (Rodent activity) is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings located on food-contact surfaces of sheet pan and along wall next to shelving in were washing area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment shall increase frequency of pest control service to twice a month. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025
Observed two outside dumpsters missing covers. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered/closed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. PIC relocated can opener to dishwasher to be washed, rinsed and sanitized. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2025

Cheese pizza, pepperoni pizza and cheeseburgers were observed held at (130-133) degrees Fahrenheit. PIC reheat pizzas (176 degrees Fahrenheit) and cheeseburgers (161 degrees Fahrenheit and 183 degrees Fahrenheit). Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2025

The presence of (rodent activity) is evidenced by rodent droppings located along the wall next to shelving in ware washing area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025
Observed walk-in freezer located in the hallway area not in good repair. Ice buildup is preventing walk-in freezer door from being able to open and close. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler ceiling was observed with an accumulation of soil residue and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2025

Observed two outside dumpsters missing covers and a few with lids not closed. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnable shall be kept covered/closed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2025
Inadequate lighting was observed in the walk-in cooler. Establishment shall replace bulb with a brighter one. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler ceiling mounted condenser unit fins were observed to have an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2025
The lighting in the walk-in cooler was observed to be very dim and in need of more intense (brighter) lightbulb. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2025

The door to the outside, located down the hall from the walk-in freezer, is not tight-fitting (bottom and top middle areas where the doors meet. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024

The door to the outside, located near the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. (The middle of the two doors has a gap.) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/20/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer has an accumulation food debris. The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in vent has an accumulation of dust and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/20/2023
